title: Review: bridge-jmap/slack/matrix/teams, wasm, ffi
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — two echo-suppression gaps (wasm/ffi) are user-visible. Cleartext IndexedDB storage is a real security exposure for the WASM client.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty: no runtime test of relay echo behavior, no WASM integration test. Would raise confidence with a live test of WASM send_message confirming no self-deliver event fires.
Confidence: 10% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: runtime WASM behavior (challenge hang scenario, IndexedDB interactions), and Teams webhook semantics not directly testable without a live Teams tenant. Would raise confidence if: integration tests existed for bridge auth handshakes and the WASM challenge timeout path. Maintainer risk: LOW — bridge code is well-structured, security-sensitive paths (HMAC verification, own-echo filtering, channel_id scoping) are all present and tested. The one new finding (teams_security_token base64 not validated at startup) is low severity. The self-oracle Teams test (nie-kef6.102) and the challenge hang (nie-li9e.3) remain the highest-priority open items in scope.
